key_facts: Spun off from JinkoSolar in late 2016; listed on Shanghai Stock Exchange (601778.SH) May 2020
Operates distributed solar EPC, O&M, power station asset management
Key signal: O&M becoming standalone financial product in China

funding_history.notes: 461x oversubscribed — highest oversubscription for a solar downstream IPO at the time

business_model_notes: Integrated downstream platform: EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) for distributed and utility-scale solar → O&M management of owned and third-party assets → green power trading (H1 2024: CNY 24.5B in green power transactions). Revenue stream diversification: construction margin + recurring O&M fee + power trading commission + PPA yield from owned plants. The CNY 24.5B green power transaction volume in H1 2024 shows Jinko Power is also a major green energy trading house, not just an installer.
competitive_position: Mid-to-large downstream solar company. CNY 4.37B revenue (2023) makes it larger than most pure-play installers. The 461x IPO oversubscription (2020) confirms market enthusiasm for O&M as a recurring-revenue model. Competes against Chint Anneng (residential focus), Sungrow New Energy (development focus), and utility-scale IPPs.
research_notes: Highest confidence (★5). All financial data from SSE (601778.SH) public annual report and H1 2024 semi-annual report. IPO details confirmed via SSE filing. Green power trading figure (CNY 24.5B) is a standout metric showing diversification into energy trading beyond pure O&M.
